title: Periloba sessiliflora
intro: Periloba sessiliflora, also known as the African or Indian Periloba, is a plant species native to tropical Africa and India, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Periloba sessiliflora include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and syrups, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
taxonomy: Periloba sessiliflora is a species of plant that belongs to the family Asclepiadaceae. This family is a part of the order Gentianales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Asclepiadaceae is now often considered a subfamily within the family Apocynaceae, indicating some reclassification. The classification of Periloba sessiliflora is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics. The exact classification details can vary depending on the source and any recent revisions in botanical taxonomy.
